title: Cover
description: DiaGe partnership proposal for Signet Jewelers
speakerNotes: Start here. Take a breath. You can say something like: 'I appreciate you taking the time. I'm [name] — I manage [your team] at Signet. Tonight I want to share an idea I've been working on that I think could change how we think about keeping our customers connected to us between purchases.' Then click to advance.

title: Act I — The Problem
description: Section divider: Act I — The Problem
speakerNotes: This is just a transition. You can simply say: 'Let me start with the problem.' Then click. The problem slide is next.

title: Customers Go Silent
description: Jewelry customers have no touchpoint with Signet between purchases
speakerNotes: This is the core insight. Walk through each bullet simply: 'Our customers buy from us, and then we don't hear from them for two or three years. We have no way to reach them. And the Diamond Bond situation — we only find out a plan has lapsed when they walk in to make a claim. By then it's too late.' Pause here. Let it land.

title: Three Gaps
description: Three specific, measurable problems that compound each other
speakerNotes: Point to each column as you speak. 'This shows up in three specific ways. First, we have no engagement layer — customer intent is invisible until they walk in. Second, right now today, 847 customers across our four banners have missed their Diamond Bond inspection window. That's $12.8 million in plan value that could lapse. Third, we have 1,847 customers actively shopping Kay, Zales, Jared, and Banter at the same time — and no single banner sees the full picture.' The $12.8M number tends to get a reaction. Give it a moment.

title: Act II — The Solution
description: Section divider: Act II — The Solution
speakerNotes: Another transition. Say: 'So here's what solves it.' Click. The product slide is next.

title: The Product
description: DiaGe — three core features that keep customers engaged between purchases
speakerNotes: Keep this one conversational. 'DiaGe is an app — but think of it as the loyalty layer we've never had. Customers use it to track the jewelry they own, build a wishlist of what they want next, and get reminded before their Diamond Bond inspection is due. The key thing is: by the time they walk into our store, they've already told us exactly what they want.' You don't need to go through every detail — the slide does that.

title: Partner Portal
description: Store associates receive three types of live purchase-intent signals
speakerNotes: This one is about the store experience. Say: 'On the store side, associates see three things: who has a wishlist saved and hasn't come in yet, who has booked an appointment and what they want, and who is overdue for a Diamond Bond inspection. All of that shows up in a simple web tool — nothing to install, no IT required. And every lead comes from the customer themselves. We don't cold-contact anyone.' The last sentence matters — it answers the privacy question before they ask it.

title: Corporate Dashboard
description: Enterprise cross-banner visibility that no individual store system can produce
speakerNotes: This is for the executive in the room. Say: 'At the corporate level, DiaGe shows us something no store system can — because it sits across all four banners. We can see which revenue came from DiaGe. We can see which customers are shopping multiple banners at once. We can see Diamond Bond risk before it becomes a claim. And we can measure how quickly customers are coming in after sharing a wishlist — which right now is three to six days, versus the fourteen to twenty-one day baseline.'

title: Store Activation
description: Print-ready QR sign drives downloads from Signet's own sales floor — zero IT required
speakerNotes: Keep this one short. 'Here's what the in-store experience looks like. Associates put this sign on the counter — or near the display case. Customer scans it, downloads DiaGe, saves their piece. From that moment forward we have a direct channel to them. No app configuration, no hardware, no IT ticket. We print it and we place it. Both variants are ready to match whichever banner's aesthetic you prefer.' Then move on — don't linger.

title: Act III — The Financial Case
description: Section divider: Act III — The Financial Case
speakerNotes: Last section. Say: 'Now let's talk about what this is worth.' Click.

title: The Revenue Mechanism
description: The exact path from wishlist to Signet sale — 100 leads generate $116K at pilot rates
speakerNotes: This is the most important slide in the deck. Speak slowly and point to each step. 'A customer saves a wishlist item. Your associate reaches out. Sixty-eight percent of them come in. Sixty percent of those buy. Average order: twenty-eight forty-seven.' Then point to the black bar at the bottom. 'Every hundred wishlist leads your stores receive generates a hundred and sixteen thousand dollars in sales at the rates we're seeing in the pilot.' Then stop. Don't explain anything else. Let them ask the follow-up.

title: KPIs vs. Industry
description: DiaGe users spend 2.3× more and convert at 5× the industry rate
speakerNotes: Walk through the table row by row. 'DiaGe users are spending $2,847 on average versus $1,240 for a typical Signet customer — that's more than double. Thirty-one percent of wishlist leads turn into a purchase, versus one to four percent for a typical retail app. Sixty-eight percent of customers we contact come in. And thirty-day retention is sixty-eight percent, versus thirty-two for the average consumer app.' If they ask where the numbers come from: 'These are from our pilot — twenty-three partner retail locations, January through May of this year.'

title: Pilot Revenue Results
description: $2.84M in attributed revenue across 4 Signet banners in the pilot period
speakerNotes: Build on the previous slide. 'And this isn't a projection — this is real. In five months, across twenty-three pilot locations, DiaGe has already generated two point eight four million dollars in sales we can directly tie back to the app. And the monthly number grew by one hundred seventy-one percent month over month.' Gesture at the bar chart. 'At full Signet scale with two thousand eight hundred locations, that's two hundred ninety million dollars in attributed revenue per year.' Pause. Let the number sit.

title: Pricing & ROI
description: At $369/month per location, break-even is 4 incremental sales per year — ~74× ROI at full rollout
speakerNotes: Keep this one light — the numbers tell the story. 'The cost is three sixty-nine a month per store. Break-even is four incremental sales per year at a fourteen hundred dollar average ticket — roughly one per quarter. At full Signet rollout across twenty-five hundred eighty-two locations, DiaGe costs eleven point four million a year and projects eight hundred forty-five million in attributed revenue. That's a seventy-four times return.' If anyone asks about the annual discount: 'Three fourteen a month if you commit annually — fifteen percent off.'

title: Full Circle
description: Every problem raised ties back to a measurable outcome — three gaps, one platform
speakerNotes: This is the moment to land it. Walk each column slowly. 'We started with three specific problems. Gap one — no shopping signal. DiaGe surfaces intent before the customer ever calls. Gap two — Diamond Bond lapses and lost paperwork. Automated reminders prevent the lapse, and the digital vault eliminates the denied-claim conversation entirely. Gap three — no cross-banner view. For the first time, corporate can see one customer across all four banners. And every one of those outcomes has a metric we'll measure together in the pilot.' Then advance to the ask.

title: Next Steps
description: 90-day pilot agreement across 25 Kay and Zales locations
speakerNotes: This is your ask. Be direct. 'Here's what I'm asking for: a signed pilot agreement for twenty-five locations across Kay and Zales. We'd be fully onboarded within two weeks. We'd report weekly against metrics we agree on together — Diamond Bond compliance rate, attributed transaction count, and time-to-appointment. At sixty days I'll bring a rollout recommendation.' Then stop. Don't fill the silence. The ask is on the slide — let them respond to it.
